tasks.text: (status board)The red here could be white for easy readability of text.

Chat reliability shipped (for the development interface): a watcher wakes the agent on every chat message (armed each session, in the start ritual for all future agents). Notifications: run complete and input needed always fire, unfocused tab counts as away, amber grant button when a browser lacks permission. Running dots clear when the server dies. 5f0697c, deployed.
Touch test results 2026-07-19 (17 of 19 passed): the findings, from his per-step notes

children.text: BIG: toggles sometimes stop working after a while (intermittent)
P1 investigation: suspect a stuck touch-gesture candidate (a pointercancel with a mismatched pointerId leaves state armed). Needs a repro harness with pointer fuzzing.

Fixed: reproduced both flavors. A render replacing the pressed row mid-press swallows its pointerup, the 1.5s hold timer arms with no finger down, and the ghost eats the next tap (dead chevron, iOS reuses pointer ids) or every scroll. Every new press now sweeps stale gesture state. Regression test pins it. (2026-07-19)
Tap on text starts editing + keyboard immediately; he wants select-first (suggested smaller text hit area)
UX decision, card sent: select-first-then-edit vs smaller edit hitbox.
Hold-drag on an UNSELECTED row acts like native text drag, no drop indicators; groups do not move as expected on touch
The native iOS text-drag grabs the gesture before ours arms on unselected rows. Fix direction: suppress native dragstart for touch presses on rows.

Fixed: the OS-native HTML5 drag (the copy-paste-looking text ghost) is suppressed for every touch press, so the 420ms hold drag owns rows AND group headers with real drop indicators. Mouse keeps native drag. Verified in browser: unselected-row moves, group reorders, desktop drag untouched. (2026-07-19)
Swipe indent: visual pull should cap at the max achievable indent level
Mechanical: compute achievable levels at gesture start, clamp the translate. In this batch.
Fixed: the pull now stops exactly at what the release can do, first tasks do not budge. (2026-07-19)
Swipe while editing closes the keyboard (confirmed from the queued item)
In this batch: restore focus to the same text after a swipe that began in an editing field.
Fixed: a swipe that starts mid-edit gives the caret and keyboard straight back. Swiping FROM the text itself now belongs to iOS text selection. (2026-07-19)
Inside FOCUSED text: no magnifier or word select, long-press goes dark
My touch guards overreach: candidates and the contextmenu block must not engage when the press starts inside focused editable text. In this batch.
Fixed: presses inside actively edited text spawn no gestures at all, magnifier and word select are native again. (2026-07-19)
Drag select worked once, then holds just shift selection instead of joining; collides with move
Real fingers drift during a 1.5s hold: the 12px cancel threshold kills the select candidate while move stays armed. Raising select tolerance in this batch, then re-grade.
Tuned: the 1.5s hold now tolerates 28px of finger drift, and swipes clear stale hold state. Re-grade with real fingers, step 8 to 11 of the plan. (2026-07-19)
Drag auto-scroll jagged, especially near the top
Polish: smooth the velocity curve. Queued behind the batch.

Fixed: four causes. Integer speed steps stuttered the slow end (now fractional with carry), the ramp slammed near the edge (now squared, gentle at the inner edge), scrollBy kept shoving into iOS rubber-banding at the top (now clamps at the end), and a gesture cleanup was killing the scroll frame mid-drag. Bonus: while auto-scroll slides content under a still finger, the drop indicator now follows the content instead of dropping on a stale row. (2026-07-19)
Desktop: mouse long-press drag selects text and opens the editor (his note: park it, do not change much)
Parked at his request.

tasks.text: Flash is still there

children.text: Written 06:10, four hours AFTER the scoped-render deploy and your tab reload, so it is real. Three suspects: your phone holding the old build, deletes (still full-render by design), or the wrapper reloading the iframe on agent writes. Card sent to pin it down; meanwhile deletes get scoped too.
Deletes now render scoped too (75ms to 14ms), the biggest remaining full-render in daily flow. Item stays open until your card answer pins where you still see flashing.

Round 2 deployed (dc526c4) from your sub-items clue: your few-huge-groups board made group scoping nearly a full render, and nested creates jumped the viewport 87 to 108px, THE flash. Rendering now swaps one task subtree: creates 150ms to 15-21ms, nested deletes 131ms to 18ms, scroll jump ZERO at every depth. You close this item when your fingers agree.

tasks.text: (new bug) When I'm typing in the first item of a group and I'm at the start of the field. If I click enter a new group is openend above and my field moves to it. When I click enter there it sohuld open new group below!

children.text: Reproducing now: Enter at the start of the first field misplacing the new row and stealing focus upward.

Fixed and deployed: Enter at the start of a field now leaves the row intact and opens the new empty item below (first child on an expanded parent), focus goes with it. The empty-row-above behavior is gone.

tasks.text: BIG ISSUE resolved (app): sync pulls past 1MB now use the blobs API, deployed (2936031). Recovery steps posted in chat: backups on both devices, then sync loser first, winner second.

children.text: My punchlist app failed to sync. now phone and laptop are not synced anymore :(
iamge of the error

Confirmed from your screenshot: the 1MB contents-API read cap, known since intake 6, now bites for real. FIXING NOW: pulls fall back to the Git blobs API (100MB ceiling) when contents returns empty. Before you sync after the fix: EXPORT a JSON backup on BOTH devices, divergence resolves local-wins and the loser survives only in git history.

tasks.text: Group top-edge drop targeting fixed (app): dropping a task on a group header lands it FIRST in the group instead of appending at the bottom (71edb26, deployed). Verified via the touch move path in a real browser.

Polish batch closed: header cluster, time-in-foot, REUSE.md, ctx chip live, history attribution via board-write.mjs, graph sketch rounds 1 and 2 delivered. The graph BUILD waits on his E/F/G pick.
Graph round 3 delivered (development interface): F refined plus ledges, switchback, and spine-hybrid variants. Stale cards from July 17 closed with reasons. Build starts on his pick.

children.text: FIXED during audit: checkbox/text overlap (the P2 squish) — root cause was grid columns vs 30px touch elements
Finding: chevron and checkbox are 30x30, color swatch 24x24 — under the 44px touch guideline (other controls already get 44 at narrow). Enlarging changes row density: needs a grill card before fixing
Your card pick built: 30px visuals with invisible 44px tap areas (pseudo-element hit boxes) on chevrons, checkboxes, and the color swatch. Hit-tested in all four directions.
Finding: clock/topbar at narrow — clock sits left in all measured widths; his 'not centered / too close to the edge' needs his intent: card sent
the issue isnt it sitting left. its its own container the clock is not ceentered and side gaps inside are incorrect.
Clean: sidebar drawer, both themes, no horizontal overflow at 390/360/617, drawer touch targets generous
Still open from P2: touch interactions themselves (indent/outdent gesture, drag select, drag-move) — design work, needs grills/prototypes
Fix batch 2 (app): focus mode keyboard parity shipped — the twice-reported P3 item. Outline arrow browsing, Ctrl+Enter toggle, Alt+move, and a guard so overlay keys never drive the hidden board.

Fix batch 3 (app): Ctrl+X cut removes at cut time, Ctrl+Up climbs to the enclosing toggle, Completed and Trash rows indent under their headers plus a narrow-width Restore-button squeeze found on the way.
Fix batch 4 (app): delete confirmation for subtrees, multi-selections, and groups, plus the scheme-aware negative-space favicon from the answered P4 spec.

Perf pass (app): arrow browsing measured at 3.5ms per press on 667 rows (not the bottleneck); pasted images now lazy-load and decode async, the likely stutter source on a screenshot-heavy board. Awaiting his retest.
Fix batch 5 (app): both card answers shipped. Backspace-at-start merge into the item above, and the left-arrow hierarchy climb. Deleting UX branch fully closed.

Touch batch (app): swipe-to-indent shipped from his card answer, distance-stepped levels with detents. Housekeeping: Playwright smoke and dead getTaskItem deleted, hasOwn modernized (368abec). Test-API pruning deferred with reason.

Touch batch 2 (app): shipped. Hold past 1.5s drag-selects, sweeps fill the range, later holds add more, move arms from anywhere on the row. Sideways viewport pan locked. Alt+Left tamed in the app and on this page (ac45ef5, 33f375b). Needs a real-phone test.

Flow of modifying and adding tasks to the graph. Currently you end up adding behind where we left off. Ideally if new things are added priority before the enxt things to be done they should be added after where we were.
ANSWERED 2026-07-17 (via card): new priority items slot in right after the active item. Now standing procedure.

Root cause found: a self-sustaining mutation loop in the wrapper (identical text writes emitting records) kept restyling every frame. Loop killed; styling now applies synchronously before paint. Confirm on your side.
The 3 running dots that show agent is active is active after agent stopped working
Two causes: I killed a heartbeat without deregistering (now a hard rule), and dots also linger up to 90 s by design after a crash so a dead agent can't show as working forever.

children.text: P1 — Flow-breaking bugs

Fixed in src/ 2026-07-17: all three creation paths (+ button, Enter on task, Enter on group) now create empty tasks. New regression test covers all paths; 85/85 tests pass; verified in an isolated browser at desktop and phone widths. Reaches both surfaces on this build.
OKAY yes everything seems to work exactly as it should!

Classified by Evren 2026-07-17: BOTH. Fix lands in src/ once, verified in the app and on the development interface (including its narrow iframe widths).

Fixed in src/ 2026-07-17: undo was resetting the selection to the board's top node and scrolling there. Selection now survives the undo (or clears if the node vanished). Bonus find: + button adds were never undoable, now they are. 86/86 tests (one new); verified in an isolated browser at desktop and narrow widths.
When you have group selected and you click enter
Behavior is not consistent
Adds item at the bottom sub level
Adds item at the bottom same level
Ideally
If the item group is open it should add a  new item at the top of those
If item group is closed it should add a new item below it at same depth
When I click enter on a grup it currently opens it and adds a new item at the top. It should just open it and keep the selection on the group
ANSWERED 2026-07-17 (via chat buttons): open group = add item at top; closed group = add item below at same depth
Classified by Evren 2026-07-17: BOTH. Fix lands in src/ once, verified in the app and on the development interface (including its narrow iframe widths).

Fixed in src/ 2026-07-17 to your spec: main group Enter opens and adds at top (unchanged); expanded parent item gets the new item as FIRST CHILD (selection path and caret-at-end editing path); collapsed parent gets a sibling below at the same depth and stays closed; mid-text splits unchanged. 87/87 tests, verified in a real browser.
P2 — Touch & phone (feeds the mobile audit)
Phone UX and UI is really bad and has missing interactions
Generally touch devices are a problem
How to do out and indent

Shipped per your card pick 2026-07-17 (7e3b9e4): horizontal swipe on a task row indents right, outdents left. Distance steps levels, one per 32px up to 3, the row clicks between detents under your finger, release commits. One undo per gesture. Vertical scroll and long-press drag keep priority: the swipe only locks after a clearly horizontal move. Needs your real thumb on the iPhone to tune the feel.
How to do drag select
Shipped 2026-07-18 (ac45ef5) per your chat spec: hold past 1.5s flips into drag select, sweep takes rows, later holds add more. Evren: the dragging works well.
How to do dragging around well
Shipped 2026-07-18 (ac45ef5): hold under 1.5s arms move from anywhere on the row, a touchmove guard stops native scroll from stealing the drag mid-move.
Test touch-screen functionality
Test plan built 2026-07-18: an 18-step checkable page, open as a tab in the test Chrome (docs/touch-test-plan.html). Stays open until you run it.

P3 — Keyboard & desktop frictions

Measured 2026-07-17 (0e12d7b): on a 667-row test board an arrow press costs 3.5ms of script, well inside the 16ms frame budget, so the handler is not the stutter. Real suspect on YOUR board: pasted screenshots decoded synchronously while scrolling. Images now lazy-load and decode async. Left OPEN: try fast browsing on your real board and tell me if it still stutters, then I trace it live.

Still hard to get to the hamburger button. And even if you are at the bottom of the tasklist when you click left you get to the hamburger button which is annoying. Also when I'M in the left menu if I go left I should also end up in the hamburger

Shipped per your spec 2026-07-17 (12c1201): left arrow climbs the hierarchy. Child to parent, top-level task to its group header, group header to the sidebar. The menu stays reachable by repeated presses, never by surprise.
creating a uppercase f puts ypu into focus mode and out maybe nice to rethink that shortcut.

Fixed in src/ 2026-07-17: AltGr on Turkish layouts reports as Ctrl+Alt, so AltGr-composed characters were toggling focus mode mid-typing. The shortcut now ignores AltGr and never fires from inside a text field. 87/87 tests; both paths browser-verified.

Keys in the overlay no longer leak to the board behind it: arrows were silently re-selecting hidden rows and Backspace could delete the board selection. 88/88 tests; browser-verified desktop and 390px.

tasks.text: General interface

children.text: (IMPORTANT) Creating an item in the board or indent outdenting an item still leads to flashing of the whole interface

DIAGNOSED 2026-07-19: create costs 75ms and indent 64ms of synchronous whole-board DOM rebuild (4499 nodes on this board), 4-5 dropped frames = the flash. The fix is scoped rendering in src/: rebuild only the affected group, not the world. It touches the app core render path and every structural caller, so it gets a fresh focused session with the full test battery, first thing next run.

FIXED and deployed (4d195e7): scoped rendering rebuilds only the group you touched. Create 75ms to 13ms, indent 64ms to 8ms on a 5010-node board, both inside one frame, the flash is structurally gone. Refresh the board to feel it.

Intake note, IMPORTANT flag kept: different from the image flash fixed earlier (that was app-side lazy loading). Create and indent rebuild the whole board DOM, suspect the full re-render or the state round trip. Gets an investigation first.

tasks.text: Engineering

children.text: Do we currently use best practices to keep the taskboard app lightweight even as +5000 items are added a lot of photos are added etc. What would we have to do . Could consult ponytail skill

Sizing facts (2026-07-19): sync writes the WHOLE board each time, git caps any file at 100MB. The real ceiling is browser localStorage at 5-10MB per origin. Text is nearly free (5000 items = ~1MB); embedded images are the entire budget (50-200KB each). Fix direction when picked up: images out of the board JSON, IndexedDB locally plus separate files in the sync repo.

GRILLED 2026-07-19 (Q21-Q23): images are a mix, both kept properly. Full eager parity, every image offline on both devices, lazy-on-view rejected. Build: board JSON sheds images to IndexedDB locally plus separate immutable files in the sync repo. Second in queue behind the flash fix.

Spec addendum from his reply 2026-07-19: assets/ not images/, subfolders allowed, any file type (videos, files, STLs later). Limits confirmed per file not per board: git hard-refuses single files over 100MB (long videos will hit this, screenshots and STLs never), repo total softly complains past a few GB. Import and export stay local, no caps.

SHIPPED v1.4.0 (app): image bytes moved out of the board. IndexedDB records locally, one immutable file each under assets/ in the sync repo, tiny references in board state. Push uploads assets before the board, pull fetches what the device is missing, each file exactly once. Existing embedded images migrate on next load, exports still embed for lossless files. The 1MB sync cap and the 5MB localStorage ceiling both retire. 108 tests, browser-verified migration, render, lightbox, captions, reload persistence.

children.text: I don't look at much than the braindump and the chat interface at this point.
Product signal worth keeping: you live in braindump plus chat. The graph earns its place or the next investment goes to those two.

history.text: Board toggle shipped: graph-only view with sync alive
Test results triaged: nine findings boarded, mechanical batch starting
Four test-plan findings fixed and deployed: swipe cap, keyboard restore, magnifier, hold tolerance
Started the dead-toggles investigation (P1): repro harness against the built app
Dead toggles fixed (app): stale touch-gesture ghosts swept on every new press
Native drag hijack fixed (app): touch presses now always use the pointer drag with drop indicators
Drag auto-scroll smoothed (app): even steps, gentle ramp, top-edge clamp, live drop target
Assets build v1.4.0 shipped (app): images now live in IndexedDB + assets/ in the sync repo
Wrapper reload retired (dev): agent edits now apply in place, selection survives
Graph zoom anchored (dev): the node under your cursor stays put, zero drift
Completed "Tap on text starts editing + keyb…"
Agent ghost chips shipped (dev): name + sign-off time, until the next session
Answer editing shipped (dev): corrections post visibly, latest answer wins
